name: Bodyweight Squat

steps.instruction: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
steps.coachingNote: Imagine standing on train tracks, not a tightrope

steps.instruction: Send hips back as if sitting in a chair
steps.coachingNote: Lead with your butt, not your knees

steps.instruction: Lower until hips are below knees
steps.coachingNote: Only go as low as feels stable

steps.instruction: Drive through heels to stand back up
steps.coachingNote: Squeeze your glutes at the top

muscles.name: Quadriceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Powers you up stairs and hills without burning out

muscles.name: Glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds the strength to stand up from a low sofa

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Protects your lower back when lifting heavy objects

modifications.name: Chair-assisted squat
modifications.description: Lightly touch a chair behind you at the bottom for balance

modifications.name: Jump squat
modifications.description: Explode up into a small jump at the top of each rep

watchOuts: Knees caving inward — push them out in line with your toes
Weight shifting to toes — keep heels glued to the floor
Rounding your lower back — keep chest proud and core tight

name: Push-Up

steps.instruction: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders
steps.coachingNote: Your body should form a straight line from head to heels

steps.instruction: Lower your chest toward the floor, elbows at 45 degrees
steps.coachingNote: Don't let your elbows flare out to the sides

steps.instruction: Stop when your chest is 1-2 inches from the ground
steps.coachingNote: Keep your core tight — don't let your hips sag

steps.instruction: Press through your palms to return to start
steps.coachingNote: Exhale as you push up

muscles.name: Chest
muscles.realLifePayoff: Makes pushing heavy doors and moving furniture easier

muscles.name: Triceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds arm strength for lifting and carrying

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.realLifePayoff: Supports overhead reaching and holding objects

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Stabilizes your body during any pushing motion

modifications.name: Knee push-up
modifications.description: Drop your knees to the ground and maintain the same upper body form

modifications.name: Diamond push-up
modifications.description: Place hands close together forming a diamond shape with thumbs and index fingers

watchOuts: Hips sagging — engage your core and keep body straight
Elbows flaring wide — keep them at 45 degrees from your body
Head dropping — maintain neutral neck alignment

name: Plank Hold

steps.instruction: Start on forearms and toes, elbows under shoulders
steps.coachingNote: Clasp hands together or keep forearms parallel

steps.instruction: Engage your core and squeeze your glutes
steps.coachingNote: Imagine pulling your belly button toward your spine

steps.instruction: Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
steps.coachingNote: Don't let your hips pike up or sag down

steps.instruction: Hold this position while breathing steadily
steps.coachingNote: If form breaks, drop to knees and continue

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Protects your spine during any bending or twisting motion

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds the endurance to hold objects overhead

muscles.name: Lower back
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens the muscles that support healthy posture

modifications.name: Knee plank
modifications.description: Drop your knees to the ground while keeping core engaged

modifications.name: Plank with leg lift
modifications.description: Lift one leg off the ground while holding the plank

watchOuts: Hips sagging — engage glutes and core
Hips too high — flatten out into a straight line
Holding your breath — breathe steadily throughout

name: Jumping Jacks

steps.instruction: Start standing with feet together and arms at your sides
steps.coachingNote: Stand tall with good posture

steps.instruction: Jump feet apart while raising arms overhead
steps.coachingNote: Land with soft knees, not locked

steps.instruction: Immediately jump feet back together while lowering arms
steps.coachingNote: Keep a steady rhythm

steps.instruction: Continue at a pace that keeps your heart rate up
steps.coachingNote: Modify to stepping side to side if needed

muscles.name: Cardiovascular system
muscles.realLifePayoff: Improves your endurance for sustained activity

muscles.name: Calves & legs
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds the power for jumping and quick movements

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens overhead reaching mobility

modifications.name: Step jacks
modifications.description: Step one foot out at a time instead of jumping

modifications.name: Plank jacks
modifications.description: Perform the leg motion in a plank position

watchOuts: Landing with locked knees — keep them soft and bent
Rushing the movement — maintain control
Poor posture — keep chest up throughout

name: Alternating Lunges

steps.instruction: Stand with feet hip-width apart
steps.coachingNote: Keep hands on hips or at your sides

steps.instruction: Step forward with one leg, lowering your hips
steps.coachingNote: Take a big enough step that your front knee stays over your ankle

steps.instruction: Lower until both knees are at 90 degrees
steps.coachingNote: Back knee should hover just above the ground

steps.instruction: Push through front heel to return to standing, then alternate
steps.coachingNote: Keep your torso upright throughout

muscles.name: Quadriceps & glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds single-leg strength for walking and climbing

muscles.name: Balance & coordination
muscles.realLifePayoff: Improves stability on uneven surfaces

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens the muscles that keep you upright

modifications.name: Assisted lunge
modifications.description: Hold onto a wall or chair for balance

modifications.name: Jumping lunges
modifications.description: Explode up and switch legs mid-air

watchOuts: Front knee passing toes — step further forward
Leaning too far forward — keep torso upright
Wobbling — engage core for stability

name: Glute Bridge

steps.instruction: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
steps.coachingNote: Place feet hip-width apart, close to your butt

steps.instruction: Press through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling
steps.coachingNote: Squeeze your glutes as you lift

steps.instruction: Hold at the top with body in a straight line from shoulders to knees
steps.coachingNote: Don't over-arch your lower back

steps.instruction: Lower back down with control
steps.coachingNote: Touch the ground briefly, then repeat

muscles.name: Glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Powers hip extension for walking up hills and stairs

muscles.name: Hamstrings
muscles.realLifePayoff: Supports your knees and lower back during movement

muscles.name: Lower back
muscles.realLifePayoff: Counteracts the effects of sitting all day

modifications.name: Shorter range
modifications.description: Lift hips only halfway if full range is difficult

modifications.name: Single-leg bridge
modifications.description: Extend one leg straight and lift with the other

watchOuts: Over-arching lower back — stop at neutral spine alignment
Pushing through toes — drive through heels
Not squeezing glutes — actively contract them at top

name: High Knees

steps.instruction: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart
steps.coachingNote: Keep your core engaged

steps.instruction: Drive one knee up toward your chest
steps.coachingNote: Aim to bring your thigh parallel to the ground

steps.instruction: Quickly switch legs in a running motion
steps.coachingNote: Stay on the balls of your feet

steps.instruction: Pump your arms in opposition to your legs
steps.coachingNote: Maintain a quick, rhythmic pace

muscles.name: Hip flexors & quads
muscles.realLifePayoff: Improves your ability to run and walk up inclines

muscles.name: Cardiovascular system
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds the stamina to sustain physical activity

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Stabilizes your body during dynamic movement

modifications.name: Marching in place
modifications.description: Slow the pace to a march and lift knees lower

modifications.name: Explosive high knees
modifications.description: Drive knees as high as possible with maximum speed

watchOuts: Leaning back — keep torso upright
Stomping feet — stay light on the balls of your feet
Swinging arms wildly — keep movement controlled

name: Burpee

steps.instruction: Start standing, then squat down and place hands on the floor
steps.coachingNote: Hands should be shoulder-width apart

steps.instruction: Jump or step feet back into a high plank position
steps.coachingNote: Keep your body in a straight line

steps.instruction: Perform a push-up by lowering your chest to the floor
steps.coachingNote: Keep your core tight — don't let your hips sag

steps.instruction: Push back up and jump or step feet forward to hands
steps.coachingNote: Land in a crouch position

steps.instruction: Jump up with arms overhead, landing softly
steps.coachingNote: You can step instead of jumping to modify

muscles.name: Full body
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds total-body power and coordination

muscles.name: Cardiovascular system
muscles.realLifePayoff: Rapidly improves your heart and lung capacity

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens stability during explosive movements

modifications.name: Step-back burpee
modifications.description: Step back one foot at a time instead of jumping, and skip the final jump

modifications.name: Burpee with push-up
modifications.description: Add a full push-up when in the plank position

watchOuts: Sagging hips in plank — keep core engaged
Landing with locked knees — absorb impact with soft knees
Rushing form — slow down if technique breaks down

name: Mountain Climbers

steps.instruction: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders
steps.coachingNote: Keep your core engaged and body straight

steps.instruction: Drive one knee toward your chest
steps.coachingNote: Keep your hips level — don't pike up

steps.instruction: Quickly switch legs, extending the bent leg back
steps.coachingNote: Think of running in place while in a plank

steps.instruction: Continue alternating at a quick pace
steps.coachingNote: Maintain plank form throughout

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds rotational stability for twisting and turning

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens the muscles that support your upper body

muscles.name: Hip flexors
muscles.realLifePayoff: Improves agility and quick direction changes

muscles.name: Cardiovascular system
muscles.realLifePayoff: Boosts heart rate for improved endurance

modifications.name: Slow mountain climbers
modifications.description: Slow the pace down to focus on form and control

modifications.name: Cross-body mountain climbers
modifications.description: Drive knee toward opposite elbow for added oblique work

watchOuts: Hips bouncing up and down — keep them level
Shoulders shifting forward — keep hands under shoulders
Poor plank form — drop to knees if form breaks

name: Tricep Dips

steps.instruction: Sit on the edge of a sturdy chair, place hands next to hips
steps.coachingNote: Fingers should point forward

steps.instruction: Slide your butt off the chair and extend legs forward
steps.coachingNote: Bend knees for easier version, straighten for harder

steps.instruction: Lower your body by bending elbows to 90 degrees
steps.coachingNote: Keep elbows pointing back, not flaring out

steps.instruction: Press through your palms to straighten arms and return to start
steps.coachingNote: Keep your back close to the chair

muscles.name: Triceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds arm strength for pushing yourself up from chairs or the floor

muscles.name: Shoulders
muscles.realLifePayoff: Supports overhead lifting and carrying

muscles.name: Chest
muscles.realLifePayoff: Assists in any pushing motion

modifications.name: Bent-knee dips
modifications.description: Keep knees bent with feet closer to the chair

modifications.name: Elevated dips
modifications.description: Place feet on another chair or elevated surface

watchOuts: Elbows flaring wide — keep them pointing back
Shoulders shrugging up — keep them down and back
Moving too far from chair — stay close to it

name: Step-Ups

steps.instruction: Stand facing a step or staircase with feet hip-width apart
steps.coachingNote: Start with a lower step if you're new to this

steps.instruction: Place your right foot fully on the step
steps.coachingNote: Make sure your entire foot is on the step, not just your toes

steps.instruction: Push through your right heel to lift your body up
steps.coachingNote: Drive with your glutes and quads, not momentum

steps.instruction: Bring left foot up to meet the right, then step down with control
steps.coachingNote: Alternate legs each rep or do all reps on one side first

muscles.name: Quadriceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Powers you up stairs and hills without fatigue

muscles.name: Glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds strength for climbing and everyday movements

muscles.name: Hamstrings
muscles.realLifePayoff: Supports knee stability and walking uphill

modifications.name: Lower step height
modifications.description: Use the first step or a lower platform

modifications.name: Explosive step-ups
modifications.description: Drive up explosively and briefly lift the back foot off the ground

watchOuts: Knee caving inward — keep it aligned with your toes
Leaning forward — keep chest up and core engaged
Using momentum — control the movement up and down

name: Stair Climbs

steps.instruction: Stand at the bottom of a staircase in an athletic stance
steps.coachingNote: Choose a flight of 10-20 steps to start

steps.instruction: Climb the stairs at a brisk pace, taking one or two steps at a time
steps.coachingNote: Land on the balls of your feet, not flat-footed

steps.instruction: Pump your arms in opposition to your legs
steps.coachingNote: Keep a steady rhythm and breathing pattern

steps.instruction: Walk down carefully to recover, then repeat
steps.coachingNote: Never run down stairs — the descent is your active recovery

muscles.name: Cardiovascular system
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds stamina for sustained physical activity

muscles.name: Legs & glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Powers you through hills and inclines without burning out

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Stabilizes your body during dynamic movement

modifications.name: Slower pace
modifications.description: Walk up at a moderate pace, focusing on consistency

modifications.name: Double steps
modifications.description: Take two steps at a time to increase intensity

watchOuts: Holding breath — maintain steady breathing throughout
Gripping handrail too tight — use lightly for balance only
Running down stairs — always walk down for safety

name: Goblet Squat

steps.instruction: Hold one dumbbell vertically at chest height with both hands
steps.coachingNote: Cup the top of the dumbbell with your palms, elbows pointing down

steps.instruction: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly out
steps.coachingNote: The weight at your chest helps you sit back naturally

steps.instruction: Lower down by sending hips back, keeping weight at chest
steps.coachingNote: Your elbows should track between your knees

steps.instruction: Drive through heels to stand back up
steps.coachingNote: The weight adds resistance and helps you learn proper depth

muscles.name: Quadriceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds leg strength for climbing stairs with groceries

muscles.name: Glutes
muscles.realLifePayoff: Powers you up from low positions while carrying weight

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Strengthens your torso to support loads safely

modifications.name: Lighter weight or bodyweight
modifications.description: Start with no weight to master form, then add a light dumbbell

modifications.name: Pause at bottom
modifications.description: Hold the bottom position for 2-3 seconds before standing

watchOuts: Knees caving inward — push them out in line with toes
Leaning too far forward — keep chest up and weight at chest level
Heels lifting — keep weight in heels throughout

name: Dumbbell Rows

steps.instruction: Hold a dumbbell in each hand, hinge forward at hips
steps.coachingNote: Keep your back flat and core tight, slight bend in knees

steps.instruction: Let arms hang straight down with palms facing each other
steps.coachingNote: You should feel a stretch in your lats

steps.instruction: Pull dumbbells up toward your ribs, leading with elbows
steps.coachingNote: Think about pulling your elbows back, not just lifting the weights

steps.instruction: Lower dumbbells back down with control
steps.coachingNote: Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top of each rep

muscles.name: Back (lats & rhomboids)
muscles.realLifePayoff: Builds pulling strength for opening heavy doors and lifting objects

muscles.name: Biceps
muscles.realLifePayoff: Supports carrying bags and holding children

muscles.name: Core
muscles.realLifePayoff: Stabilizes your spine when bending and lifting

modifications.name: Single-arm row with support
modifications.description: Place one hand on a chair for support, row one arm at a time

modifications.name: Slow tempo rows
modifications.description: Take 3 seconds to lift, pause, then 3 seconds to lower

watchOuts: Rounding your back — keep spine neutral throughout
Using momentum — control the weight, don't swing it
Shrugging shoulders — keep shoulders down and back
